body: Compared local gastown and dun with current ntm, claude-squad, dmux, creack/pty, go-expect, and asciinema. Conclusion: tmux is useful operator prior art but creates ambiguous service/cassette evidence. ADR-002 now says no tmux in core harness execution, quota/model probes, cassettes, cancellation, or inspection; existing tmux helpers are legacy diagnostics only. Queued direct PTY implementation beads agent-949a5ba4, agent-c3155c52, and agent-2e7c356c.
summary: Re-reviewed tmux/direct PTY decision and amended ADR-002 to require direct PTY only

body: Folded the review into ADR-002, harness golden integration docs, and follow-up beads. The plan now separates internal/pty/session, internal/pty/terminal, internal/pty/cassette, and harness-specific probe adapters; forbids internal/pty imports from internal/harnesses; adds Docker TUI conformance starting with Unix top over multiple screens plus pager/editor-style TUIs; adds cassette recorder/replay work; strengthens Claude/Codex quota and model-list beads with replay and authenticated recorder coverage; and adds a token-usage bead for Claude/Codex.
summary: Reviewed PTY plan for module boundaries and robust conformance coverage

body: Updated ADR-002 and follow-up beads so cassette record/playback is a first-class internal/pty library layer rather than a side helper. Version-1 cassettes now carry manifest.timing with default resolution_ms=100; input/frame/service-event records carry monotonic t_ms; replay supports realtime, scaled, and collapsed modes. Reconciled harness golden artifact names with ADR-002 and kept service events opaque below the cassette boundary.
summary: Folded cassette record/playback into PTY library

body: Ran a throwaway Go spike using creack/pty v1.1.24 and hinshun/vt10x against /usr/bin/top. The spike started top under direct PTY, captured 16,714 raw bytes, rendered four frames, sent input '1' to change the screen, resized the PTY/model to 100x30, and exited with 'q'. Result: direct PTY control is tractable, but raw output contains dense ANSI control sequences, so ADR-003 now requires a real terminal emulator behind internal/pty/terminal rather than regex stripping.
